title: Smoky Dense Algerian Lentil Stew
description: A hearty, smoky lentil stew infused with tarragon leaves and a subtle fermented note, perfect for a comforting Algerian dinner.
instructions: Rinse the lentils under cold water until the water runs clear and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the diced carrot and celery, and sauté for 4 minutes until they start to soften.
Mix in the tomato paste, oregano, and smoked paprika, stirring until the tomatoes are well coated.
Pour in the lentils, canned tomatoes, vegetable broth, and fermented soy sauce; bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and the stew has thickened, stirring occasionally.
Add the tarragon leaves and season with salt and pepper to taste. Let the stew simmer for an additional 5 minutes to meld the flavors.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with a drizzle of olive oil or fresh parsley.
